title: The Gang Solves the Gas Crisis

jokes.text: Well, because, you know, electricity's so expensive, man. So I figured let's get a generator and, you know, run the bar on it.
jokes.setup: Charlie explains his logic for using a gas generator to save money
jokes.punchline: The obvious illogic that gasoline is far more expensive than electricity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie moment - confidently delivering terrible logic. The character integration is excellent as only Charlie would make this decision.

jokes.text: You're not the decision-making type. / As the brains of this organization, I should've made this decision. / I'm sorry but when did you become the brains? / I'm sorry I've always been the brains.
jokes.setup: Mac criticizes Charlie for making bad decisions
jokes.punchline: Immediate power struggle over who is 'the brains' with competing claims
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing the gang's delusional self-perception and immediate ego conflicts. The rapid-fire escalation works well.

jokes.text: Are you kidding me? Gasoline's, like, a thousand times more expensive than electricity.
jokes.setup: Charlie thinks he's saving money with the generator
jokes.punchline: Dennis pointing out the obvious flaw in Charlie's 'brilliant' plan
jokes.explanation: Strong setup/payoff showing Charlie's complete lack of basic logic

jokes.text: What are your talking about? I thought I was the brains. What the hell am I? / You're the looks. / Hey of course I'm the looks, but I was seeing myself as the brains and the looks.
jokes.setup: Dennis realizes he's not considered the brains
jokes.punchline: His immediate acceptance of being 'the looks' while still wanting to be both
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis moment - his vanity about his looks combined with his need to be everything. The character integration is spot-on.

jokes.text: No, you're the looks, I'm the brains, Charlie's the wild card. / Oh! That's awesome. / Yeah! Yeah, that's the classic setup.
jokes.setup: Mac assigns roles to everyone
jokes.punchline: Charlie's genuine excitement about being labeled the 'wild card'
jokes.explanation: Charlie's childlike enthusiasm for being the 'wild card' is perfectly in character and genuinely funny. Sets up the running gag beautifully.

jokes.text: Look, every great crew in history has followed that basic dynamic, right? Looks, brains, wild card. Think about it. The A-Team did it. Scooby Doo did it. The Ghost busters did it.
jokes.setup: Mac explains his theory about successful teams
jokes.punchline: Applying serious business logic to cartoon characters and action shows
jokes.explanation: The absurd application of pop culture analysis to justify their dysfunction is classic Sunny humor.

jokes.text: We're getting plowed in the ass by the oil companies and the gas companies with their ten-gallon hats and their rotten, ass-plowing hearts.
jokes.setup: Mac begins to explain their economic situation
jokes.punchline: Mixing crude sexual metaphors with cartoonish villain imagery
jokes.explanation: Crude but fits Mac's character. The mixed metaphors are absurd but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: It involves pulling up our bootstraps, oiling up a couple of asses and doing a little plowing of our own. Pow! / Not gay sex. / Okay. / Okay, 'cause that's what it sounded like.
jokes.setup: Mac uses business metaphors that sound sexual
jokes.punchline: Having to immediately clarify he didn't mean gay sex
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection joke that works because of the characters' obliviousness to how they sound.

jokes.text: It involves pulling up our bootstraps, oiling up a couple of asses and doing a little plowing of our own. Pow!
jokes.setup: Mac building up his brilliant business plan
jokes.punchline: His plan sounds extremely sexual before he clarifies
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - Mac's business metaphors sound like a plan for group sex

jokes.text: Not gay sex. Okay. Okay, 'cause that's what it sounded like.
jokes.setup: Mac's elaborate metaphor about oiling and plowing
jokes.punchline: Dennis's deadpan clarification of what everyone was thinking
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Mac's pretentious metaphor with Dennis's matter-of-fact response

jokes.text: Yeah, man, if the guy doesn't seem like he's going for it, I'll show him this, uh, big-breasted women chart... and that'll win him over.
jokes.setup: Charlie suggests a backup plan for the loan meeting
jokes.punchline: His confidence that a chart of breasts will seal a business deal
jokes.explanation: Pure Charlie logic - completely inappropriate but delivered with total confidence.

jokes.text: Charlie makes an incomprehensible wild card face
jokes.setup: Charlie tries to demonstrate his 'wild card' facial expression
jokes.punchline: His face is so bizarre the others can't even interpret it
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy that works because of Charlie's commitment and the others' genuine confusion.

jokes.text: And you want him to think that you're going to take a dump or...? / Seriously, that's what that looks like?
jokes.setup: Dennis tries to interpret Charlie's facial expression
jokes.punchline: His interpretation that Charlie looks like he needs to defecate
jokes.explanation: Solid reaction beat that extends the visual gag about Charlie's facial expressions.

jokes.text: It's not a rape van. It's a spy van. / It looks very much like a rape van to me but whatever.
jokes.setup: Frank defends his van's appearance
jokes.punchline: Dennis's casual acceptance that it does look like a rape van
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy - the casual discussion of something deeply inappropriate. Dennis's deadpan delivery sells it.

jokes.text: I was going to eliminate everybody else and be the only surviving heir, so... help. / So when you say 'eliminate,' you mean you were going to kill me? / I don't know. No, yes, maybe I was going to; maybe I wasn't.
jokes.setup: Dee casually mentions her inheritance plan
jokes.punchline: Her casual admission to planning murder followed by indecisiveness
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy with perfect Dee characterization - casually admitting to murder plots while being wishy-washy about it.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, I feel like you're really focused on one tiny part of what I'm trying to talk about here. / It's a very big part of your... / It's just a couple of words.
jokes.setup: Dennis is upset about Dee's murder plans
jokes.punchline: Dee treating planned murder as a minor detail - 'just a couple of words'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Dee's complete lack of empathy and perspective. The minimization of murder is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Wait for it... / Gasoline. / Excuse me. / Don't rush me. / Don't rush him.
jokes.setup: The loan officer asks why they need money
jokes.punchline: Mac's dramatic buildup to the obvious answer 'gasoline'
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing their delusion about how impressive their plan is.

jokes.text: These are gorgeous women with heaving breasts. / Yeah. Why? / Well, to be perfectly honest, we sort of thought we'd be speaking to a man today, so...
jokes.setup: Charlie explains what's on their 'business' chart
jokes.punchline: Their assumption that breasts would work on a male loan officer
jokes.explanation: Good callback that makes their sexism even more apparent and ridiculous.

jokes.text: My boss is a woman. / Really? / Your boss is a woman? / Now, this is a strange bank.
jokes.setup: The loan officer reveals her boss is also a woman
jokes.punchline: Their conclusion that this makes it a 'strange bank'
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of their backwards worldview - women in leadership positions being 'strange' to them.

jokes.text: You know... I just had a crazy thought. How's about I take you in the back and 'change' your mind?
jokes.setup: Dennis tries to seduce the loan officer
jokes.punchline: His crude and inappropriate sexual proposition in a business setting
jokes.explanation: Classic Dennis move - his delusional confidence in his sexual appeal. The air quotes around 'change' make it worse.

jokes.text: Or how about we all go in the back and have great sex? / What are you doing, hmm? / I'm pulling a wild card here, man, so...
jokes.setup: Dennis tries to seduce the loan officer
jokes.punchline: Charlie escalating to group sex as his 'wild card' move
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation and callback. Charlie's interpretation of being the 'wild card' is hilariously inappropriate.

jokes.text: You can't pull the wild card when I already have my shirt off, that should be a rule. Can that be a rule? / Yes, that's a rule. / Oh, yeah?! Oh, yeah?! Back off! / Wild card, baby!
jokes.setup: The gang argues about protocol during their failed seduction
jokes.punchline: Charlie ignoring the newly established rule and going full wild card
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and one of the most quotable lines. Charlie's commitment to being unpredictable is perfect character work.

jokes.text: I water boarded some guy. Told me everything. / You tortured somebody? / Oh, yeah. Big time.
jokes.setup: Frank explains how he got information
jokes.punchline: His casual admission to torture and pride about it
jokes.explanation: Classic Frank moment - casually admitting to war crimes with enthusiasm. The 'big time' emphasizes his pride.

jokes.text: I don't want anybody to see in. / We can't see out, Frank! / I might've overdone it a little bit.
jokes.setup: Frank explains why he tinted the van windows
jokes.punchline: The windows are so dark they can't see anything
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag that shows Frank's tendency to overdo everything. His understatement is perfect.

jokes.text: the gas pedal's stuck or something. / Well, wobble it. / 'Wobble it'? / Yeah, wobble it back and forth. / Wiggle it. Wiggle it.
jokes.setup: The van has mechanical problems
jokes.punchline: Frank's technical advice is to 'wobble' and 'wiggle' the pedal
jokes.explanation: Frank's mechanical incompetence shown through childish language. Decent character moment.

jokes.text: Hey, assholes.
jokes.setup: The gang crashes into someone
jokes.punchline: The victim's immediate, justified anger
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat that sets up the consequences of their incompetence.

jokes.text: I can't believe Dee keeps her life savings in her sock drawer. That's a dumb place to hide it. She's an investor now. She'll thank us later.
jokes.setup: Mac explains taking Dee's money
jokes.punchline: His rationalization that theft is actually helping her become an 'investor'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Mac's ability to rationalize terrible behavior as helpful.

jokes.text: I kind of can't believe that you guys took your shirts off back there, man. I mean, I feel like you really sold me down the river. The Ghostbusters would never do something like that to each other.
jokes.setup: Mac expresses disappointment in his friends
jokes.punchline: Using Ghostbusters as a moral authority on teamwork
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that shows Mac genuinely believes in his pop culture analysis. The wounded tone is perfect.

jokes.text: Stop now. You can't do this. / Come on, man. / Only cars. / So help me God, I'll blow this place to kingdom come.
jokes.setup: Gas station attendant won't let them fill containers
jokes.punchline: Charlie immediately threatening terrorism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie escalation - going from zero to terrorism threat over a minor inconvenience.

jokes.text: Plus I have swallowed a good deal of it. It's just a damn waste.
jokes.setup: Discussion of their gas operation problems
jokes.punchline: Charlie casually mentioning he's been drinking gasoline
jokes.explanation: Brilliant throwaway line that's pure Charlie - treating gasoline consumption as a minor inconvenience. Perfect economy and character work.

jokes.text: You're about to experience the hard knocks of a free market, bitch. Get ready to feel it where it hurts. / Your dick. / No, no, not his dick. His wallet. / Your wallet.
jokes.setup: Mac threatens economic competition
jokes.punchline: Charlie's misunderstanding followed by correction to 'wallet'
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that plays on Charlie's tendency to think literally and sexually.

jokes.text: Take this fertilizer, toss it in the closet. / What the hell are we doing that for? / 'Cause when I call the cops on him, they'll come and find all this bomb-making shit and they'll arrest his money-stealing ass.
jokes.setup: Frank explains his plan to frame Bruce
jokes.punchline: Casually planning to plant evidence and frame someone for terrorism
jokes.explanation: Classic Frank criminal behavior presented matter-of-factly. The casual attitude toward framing someone is darkly funny.

jokes.text: That's a baby monitor, Frank. / You're planting a baby monitor? / Yeah? / A lot of people are bugging their babies these days. / I guess babies can't be trusted.
jokes.setup: Frank reveals his surveillance equipment
jokes.punchline: His rationale that babies need to be monitored because they can't be trusted
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd logic delivered deadpan. The idea that babies are inherently suspicious is perfectly Frank.

jokes.text: Like maybe Bruce is banging dudes. / Why would that be shady? / Maybe the dudes are babies.
jokes.setup: Frank speculates about what they might discover
jokes.punchline: Escalating from gay sex to pedophilia as if it's logical progression
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation that fits Frank's character but is more shocking than clever.

jokes.text: Like maybe Bruce is banging dudes. Why would that be shady? Maybe the dudes are babies.
jokes.setup: Frank speculating about what they'll discover
jokes.punchline: Frank's casual escalation from adultery to pedophilia
jokes.explanation: Frank's disturbing escalation shows his twisted thinking

jokes.text: How exactly are you planning on counting a liquid? / I know how to count, dude.
jokes.setup: Dennis questions Mac's business plan
jokes.punchline: Mac's complete misunderstanding of the problem with counting liquids
jokes.explanation: Good absurd moment showing Mac's confidence despite not understanding basic concepts.

jokes.text: I, for one, suggest me blowing fireballs to get that attention. / Fireballs? / Where is that coming from?
jokes.setup: Discussion of how to attract customers
jokes.punchline: Charlie's random suggestion of breathing fire
jokes.explanation: Pure Charlie randomness - suggesting dangerous performance art for their gas business.

jokes.text: Wow. Okay, wow, I take back everything I just said.
jokes.setup: Dennis opposing Charlie's fireball idea
jokes.punchline: Dennis being impressed after Charlie successfully does it
jokes.explanation: Dennis's complete reversal shows how Charlie's chaos sometimes works

jokes.text: Goddamn it! You burned my head! / Oh, I did. I burned him. / Really? / Easy, camper, easy, camper.
jokes.setup: Charlie's fireball goes wrong
jokes.punchline: Charlie's casual acknowledgment that he burned Dennis
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy payoff with Charlie's typical lack of concern for others' wellbeing.

jokes.text: Do you have anything other than a towel and duct tape? Not really.
jokes.setup: Dennis needs medical treatment for burns
jokes.punchline: Mac's inadequate first aid supplies
jokes.explanation: Mac's primitive approach to serious medical emergencies

jokes.text: we're going to be done with your plans for a little while. 'Cause the system you set up, it ain't working.
jokes.setup: Dennis injured from following Mac's plan
jokes.punchline: Dennis taking control after being literally burned
jokes.explanation: Dennis's narcissistic takeover after injury gives him excuse

jokes.text: What happened was, we confused your bossiness with braininess, and those two are very different things.
jokes.setup: Dennis explains why Mac's leadership failed
jokes.punchline: His pseudo-intellectual analysis while being equally terrible himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis moment - his condescending intellectualism while being equally incompetent.

jokes.text: We're going to steal Frank's rape van because driving around with all that gasoline is ruing the interior of my car, and I just... I won't have it.
jokes.setup: Dennis explains their new plan
jokes.punchline: His primary concern being damage to his car's interior
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis vanity - more concerned about his car than their dangerous scheme.

jokes.text: Why would we need disguises, Charlie? / So people don't know who we are, you know. / They already don't know who we are.
jokes.setup: Charlie suggests disguises for their door-to-door sales
jokes.punchline: Dennis pointing out they're already anonymous
jokes.explanation: Excellent logic joke with perfect economy. Dennis's deadpan delivery of the obvious truth is great.

jokes.text: I bet you still hide your money in your sock drawer. / My sock drawer. / Yeah. / Come on. That would be stupid. / Yeah, you do.
jokes.setup: Dee tries to deny her money storage method
jokes.punchline: Frank immediately knowing she's lying and her transparent reaction
jokes.explanation: Good callback that reveals Dee's terrible poker face and predictability.

jokes.text: I bet you still hide your money in your sock drawer. My sock drawer. Yeah. Come on. That would be stupid. Yeah, you do.
jokes.setup: Frank accusing Dee of poor security habits
jokes.punchline: Dee accidentally confirming then trying to backtrack
jokes.explanation: Classic verbal trap showing Dee's lack of criminal sophistication

jokes.text: You're setting me up, and I'm getting the hell out. Frank, get back here. You're not gonna do this to me, girl.
jokes.setup: Frank becomes paranoid during surveillance
jokes.punchline: Frank's paranoid meltdown and calling Dee 'girl'
jokes.explanation: Frank's paranoia turning on Dee shows his unstable nature

jokes.text: Hello, ma'am. Well, what a lovely housedress. / Yeah, well, you're looking all sorts of good. / Now, you seem like a sweet, sophisticated, nice, busy, young lady, so we're not gonna waste your time today.
jokes.setup: The gang attempts door-to-door sales in disguise
jokes.punchline: Their terrible attempts at charm and professionalism
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing their complete inability to be normal salespeople.

jokes.text: Nah. We're just a couple oil men in from Dallas, and, well, we're itching like a hound to give you a-something you want.
jokes.setup: Mac continuing his oil man persona
jokes.punchline: His terrible fake accent and creepy phrasing
jokes.explanation: Mac's awful Southern impression makes him sound more threatening than charming

jokes.text: Nah. We're just a couple oil men in from Dallas, and, well, we're itching like a hound to give you a-something you want. / We want to feel you up, if'n you were so inclined as to let us.
jokes.setup: Mac tries to maintain their oil businessman cover
jokes.punchline: Charlie immediately propositioning sexual assault
jokes.explanation: Classic Charlie escalation that destroys any pretense of legitimacy in their scheme.

jokes.text: Hells, yeah! We want to feel you up, if'n you were so inclined as to let us.
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to make a legitimate sales pitch
jokes.punchline: Charlie immediately propositioning sexual assault in fake accent
jokes.explanation: Perfect wild card moment - Charlie completely derailing their plan with sexual harassment

jokes.text: Well, now you're just talking like Foghorn Leghorn! / I say, I say, that's just damn preposterous, boy.
jokes.setup: Dennis calls out Charlie's accent
jokes.punchline: Charlie doubling down with an even more cartoonish voice
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Charlie's voice work with a specific cartoon reference that lands well.

jokes.text: Okay, okay, I admit it! / What do you admit?! / I admit anything you want to hear! / Now, you admit that Bruce Mathis is a terrorist... / He's a terrorist! / and that you are working for him! / Yes, I work for him. We get our information from the top, the top cave.
jokes.setup: Frank waterboards Dee for information
jokes.punchline: Dee desperately agreeing to any accusation, including working from a 'top cave'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how torture produces false confessions. The 'top cave' is a nice absurd detail.

jokes.text: All right, admit it, that Muslim guy was gonna rub me out, and that's when you jumped out of the van after I got out!
jokes.setup: Frank's paranoid conspiracy theories
jokes.punchline: Frank's elaborate paranoid theory about assassination attempt
jokes.explanation: Frank's paranoid delusions about coordinated attacks

jokes.text: Frank is the muscle. / What? / The reason that shit hasn't been working out for us is because we are not working with our full crew. I'm the brains, you're the looks, Charlie's the wild card, and Frank is the muscle. / Well, what's Dee? / She's the useless chick.
jokes.setup: Dennis has a realization about their team dynamic
jokes.punchline: Categorizing Dee as 'the useless chick'
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that completes their team dynamic while perfectly capturing their sexism toward Dee.

jokes.text: She's the useless chick. Yeah!
jokes.setup: Assigning roles to complete their crew
jokes.punchline: Dee's role being openly described as 'useless' with enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures their dismissive treatment of Dee with brutal honesty

jokes.text: I got Dee to admit to things she never did! / Well, then it works.
jokes.setup: Frank brags about his torture effectiveness
jokes.punchline: Dennis accepting false confessions as proof the method works
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy about torture and false confessions. Dennis's logic is perfectly twisted and delivered deadpan.

jokes.text: Because I cut the brakes! Wild card, bitches!
jokes.setup: Everyone panicking about the van not stopping
jokes.punchline: Charlie revealing he sabotaged their own vehicle as his wild card move
jokes.explanation: Perfect culmination of Charlie's wild card arc - completely self-destructive chaos delivered with iconic line

jokes.text: Because I cut the brakes! Wild card, bitches!
jokes.setup: The van's brakes don't work as they're driving
jokes.punchline: Charlie's revelation that he sabotaged their own vehicle
jokes.explanation: Perfect culmination of Charlie's 'wild card' character arc. The timing, delivery, and complete insanity make this legendary. Pure chaos embodied.

jokes.text: I didn't do anything to anybody, man! I love that car!
jokes.setup: The gang crashes into an innocent person's car
jokes.punchline: The victim's genuine distress and confusion about why this happened to him
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat showing the collateral damage of the gang's schemes. The innocent victim's pain is darkly funny.

jokes.text: If there's something strange / In your neighborhood / Who you gonna call? / Ghostbusters!
jokes.setup: Episode ends with chaos and failure
jokes.punchline: Ghostbusters theme playing as ironic commentary on their incompetence
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic ending that calls back to Mac's team theory while highlighting their complete failure to be heroes like the Ghostbusters.

tmdb_overview: Charlie, Mac, and Dennis formulate a plan to take advantage of the rising gas prices. By purchasing excess amounts now, they can sell it in the future and profit. As usual, things don't go as planned. Dee is also out to make money when she learns that Bruce is going to give the family fortune to a Muslim cultural center. She and Frank try to stop him and get the cash for themselves.
